mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point signals an imminent labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict return direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumption and investment channels, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that translates into higher short-term equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns during recovery phases or signal direction flips entirely outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth trends show no predictive power for 20-day returns or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 235.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening to curb inflation, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL breaches above 235.0 but forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances or inflation threshold fails to predict return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and GDP expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output, but when both series expand jointly, it confirms broad economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to predict positive returns or interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales indicate weakening consumer demand that immediately pressures corporate earnings guidance, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines produce positive forward returns during tightening cycles or signal loses predictive accuracy outside this specific regime.
